summary: Test suite violates the core requirement that ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT. Unit tests via bats (matrix-gen.bats, workflow-structure.bats) run directly outside the workflow, not through the pipeline. Only 4 integration test cases run via act-harness.sh. Bats tests cover basic functionality (dimensions, include, exclude, limits, validation) but miss edge cases tested in the code itself (malformed include/exclude types, special characters, negative values). Act-harness uses loose substring pattern matching (grep -F) rather than exact value assertions—it checks that patterns like '"max-parallel": 4' appear in output, but doesn't validate the complete JSON structure or multiple invocation results. Workflow structure tests exist but are minimal. While individual tests are well-named and fixtures are externalized, the fundamental architecture fails the "all tests through act" mandate, limiting confidence in catching regressions at the integration level.
